_instructions.slide_naming: slide-01.jpg, slide-02.jpg ... (exported from PowerPoint → File → Export → JPEG/PNG)
_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder for embedded PDF viewer

title: Loops Concept
subtitle: Repeat Tasks Efficiently with Python!
overview: Students learn how loops help computers repeat tasks automatically. They explore for loops and while loops, work with counters and lists, and create simple programs that perform repetitive actions without writing the same code multiple times.

images._note: Add screenshots of loop examples, counters, lists, and student coding activities.

youtubeVideos.title: Python For Loops for Beginners
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how to repeat actions using for loops.

youtubeVideos.title: Python While Loops Explained
youtubeVideos.desc: Understand while loops and loop conditions.

youtubeVideos.title: Python Lists and Loops
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how loops work with lists.

activity.title: Create Loop-Based Programs
steps: Write a for loop that prints numbers from 1 to 10
Run the program and observe the output
Create a while loop that counts from 1 to 5
Display the counter value each time
Use a for loop to print each fruit name
Modify the loop to print item numbers
Discuss real-life examples of repetitive tasks

quiz.q: What is the main purpose of a loop?
quiz.explanation: Loops allow programs to repeat instructions automatically.

quiz.q: Which loop is commonly used when the number of repetitions is known?
quiz.explanation: A for loop is ideal when the number of repetitions is known.

quiz.q: Which loop continues until a condition becomes False?
quiz.explanation: A while loop runs as long as its condition is True.

quiz.q: What is a counter in a loop?
quiz.explanation: Counters keep track of how many times a loop has executed.

quiz.q: Which data structure stores multiple values?
quiz.explanation: Lists store multiple values in a single variable.

quiz.q: What happens in an infinite loop?
quiz.explanation: An infinite loop keeps running because its condition never becomes False.

quiz.q: Which keyword starts a while loop?
quiz.explanation: The while keyword is used to create a while loop.

quiz.q: What is the output count of range(3)?
quiz.explanation: range(3) produces 0, 1, and 2, which are three values.
Loops save time and make programs shorter and more efficient.
